Play-level grade distribution

PFF 0-100 grades are derived from a detailed -2 to +2 grading scale applied to every player on every play. For more information on PFF’s grading process, click here.

Big-time throw rate vs turnover-worthy play rate

Positively graded vs negatively graded play rate

Stable & unstable metrics

Percentile rank vs qualified quarterbacks

Stable metrics

The most stable facets of quarterback play are those with the biggest sample sizes — even though they intuitively seem “easier.” When projecting a quarterback’s future output, their performance from a clean pocket, on early downs and with no play action should be weighted heavily, and these are strong components when projecting PFF’s Wins Above Replacement metric. Good quarterbacks dominate these situations, while lesser quarterbacks rank near the bottom of the league. Negatively graded plays are also highly correlated from year to year, meaning the quarterback is largely in control of their negatives, while their positive plays may fluctuate due to supporting cast and play calling.

Clean-pocket grade
67
Standard-dropback grade
67
Grade on 1st & 2nd down
73
Grade with no play-action
64
Negatively graded play rate
67

Unstable metrics

Unstable metrics are more volatile when it comes to projecting quarterback play, and results tend to vary from year to year. These numbers are more descriptive than predictive, even if they’re perceived as more important. Quarterbacks see unstable situations less often, and that results in the volatility in performance. Positively graded throws are less stable than negatives; they tend to fluctuate more from year to year because they are more dependent on surrounding talent, play calling and overall supporting cast. If a quarterback has an exceptional season in unstable metrics, buyer beware — it is difficult to duplicate.

Positively graded throw rate
57

4

Defenders dropped 4 would-be INTs (3 on bad throws, 1 on good). Those are not in the model above — extra luck on top of the -2.0.

League-average modeling expected about 10.0 picks (5.9 + 4.1). This QB threw 12 (9 + 3) — roughly -2.0 more than expected on throw quality alone.
